2605.15044.title: SpeakerLLM: A Speaker-Specialized Audio-LLM for Speaker Understanding and Verification Reasoning
2605.15044.paper_summary: As audio-first agents become increasingly common in physical AI, conversational robots, and screenless wearables, audio large language models (audio-LLMs) must integrate speaker-specific understanding to support user authorization, personalization, and context-aware interaction. This requires modeling who is speaking, how the voice sounds, and how recording conditions affect speaker cues. Conventional speaker verification systems provide strong scalar scores but little linguistic evidence, while current audio-LLMs and speaker-aware language models have limited ability to organize speaker information beyond binary labels or descriptive profiles. We present SpeakerLLM, a speaker-specialized audio-LLM framework that unifies single-utterance speaker profiling, recording-condition understanding, utterance-pair speaker comparison, and evidence-organized verification reasoning within a natural-language interface. We construct verification-reasoning targets and a decision-composition policy that separate profile-level evidence from the final same-or-different decision and organize recording condition, profile evidence, and the decision into a structured trace. At its core, SpeakerLLM uses a hierarchical speaker tokenizer designed to capture multiple granularities of speaker evidence. Utterance-level speaker embeddings summarize identity and profile-level cues, whereas frame-level speaker features preserve fine-grained acoustic descriptors. Experiments show that SpeakerLLM-Base improves speaker-profile and recording-condition understanding over general audio-LLMs, while SpeakerLLM-VR preserves strong generated-verdict accuracy and produces decision traces grounded in the supervised verification reasoning schema. We will release the metadata-enriched supervision dataset and target-construction code for reproducibility.
2605.15044.paper_summary_zh: 隨著以音頻為主的代理在物理人工智慧、對話機器人和無屏幕可穿戴設備中變得越來越普遍，音頻大型語言模型（audio-LLMs）必須整合特定說話者的理解，以支持用戶授權、個性化和情境感知的互動。這需要建模誰在說話、聲音聽起來怎樣，以及錄音條件如何影響說話者的提示。傳統的說話者驗證系統提供強大的標量分數，但幾乎沒有語言證據，而當前的音頻-LLMs和說話者感知語言模型在組織說話者信息方面的能力有限，僅限於二元標籤或描述性檔案。我們提出了SpeakerLLM，一個專門針對說話者的音頻-LLM框架，統一了單次發話者檔案建模、錄音條件理解、發話對比說話者比較和證據組織的驗證推理，並在自然語言介面中實現。 我們構建了驗證推理目標和決策組合政策，將檔案級證據與最終的同或不同決策分開，並將錄音條件、檔案證據和決策組織成結構化的痕跡。SpeakerLLM的核心使用了一種層次化的說話者標記器，旨在捕捉多種細粒度的說話者證據。發話級的說話者嵌入總結了身份和檔案級提示，而幀級說話者特徵則保留了細緻的聲學描述符。實驗顯示，SpeakerLLM-Base在說話者檔案和錄音條件理解上優於一般的音頻-LLMs，而SpeakerLLM-VR則保持強大的生成裁決準確性，並生成基於監督驗證推理架構的決策痕跡。我們將釋出富含元數據的監督數據集和目標構建代碼，以便重現。
2605.15044.author: KiHyun Nam et.al.
2605.15044.authors: KiHyun Nam, Jungwoo Heo, Siu Bae, Ha-Jin Yu, Joon Son Chung
